mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) reflect improving consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings and equity prices higher over the following month. Persistent increases in RSAFS signal expansion in the consumption component of GDP, directly supporting SPX forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS 3-month log change against 20-day forward SPX returns is less than 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window, or the long-short decile spread is not significantly positive at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.5 percentage points over a three-month period indicates accelerating labor market tightness, which boosts wage growth and consumer spending, leading to positive equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines more than 0.5pp over 3 months
failure_condition: In periods triggered by the 0.5pp/3m UNRATE decline, the average 60-day forward SPX return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p>0.10) or is less than the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low or falling federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) and rising housing starts (HOUST) jointly signals accommodative monetary conditions and improving real estate activity. This interaction amplifies positive sentiment and credit availability, driving equity prices higher over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below its trailing 12-month average and HOUST is above its trailing 12-month average
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS z-score * HOUST z-score) has insignificant predictive power for 20-day returns in a linear regression (p>0.05), or the average return in the joint-signal regime is not significantly above the regime-average return (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During the post-recession recovery phase, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust manufacturing activity and supply-side expansion. This sustained production growth leads to higher corporate profits and drives the S&P 500 higher over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery (INDPRO rising from cyclical trough)
failure_condition: In months when INDPRO rises >0.5% and the economy is within 2 years of a recession end (per NBER dating), the 20-day forward SPX return is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) or the win rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: The level of the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) acts as a persistent valuation headwind: higher rates increase the discount rate on future equity cash flows, compressing price-to-earnings multiples and depressing short-term equity returns. The mechanism is always active as rates adjust capital allocation.
failure_condition: The correlation between FEDFUNDS level and 5-day forward SPX returns is not significantly negative (one-tailed test p>0.10), or the decile spread (low vs high rate) produces an insignificant long-short return (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of declining real GDP (GDPC1) is a classic recession signal. The market prices in deteriorating corporate profits during such contractions, leading to negative equity returns over the following 60 trading days. This mechanism switches off when GDP growth is positive.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 contracts (negative growth) for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: During and just after officially dated recessions (NBER), 60-day SPX forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p>0.10) or the rule triggers fewer than 3 distinct episodes in the available history.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive momentum in both nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) and real retail sales (RSAFS) provides a comprehensive signal of labor market health and consumer spending strength. The joint confirmation reduces noise and reliably predicts positive equity returns over a one-month horizon, as both indicators reflect synchronized economic expansion.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS and RSAFS show positive 1-month growth above their respective 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: The joint signal (both indicators above their 12-month MA) does not produce an average 20-day forward return significantly greater than the baseline unconditional return (t-test p>0.10), or the hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6%, each monthly decline in UNRATE signals that the labor market is healing from a distressed condition. In this high-unemployment regime, falling joblessness strongly boosts consumer confidence and expected earnings, leading to above-average equity returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 6% and decreasing month-over-month, the average 20-day forward SPX return is not significantly greater than the average return when UNRATE > 6% but not decreasing (t-test p>0.10), or the regime-conditional signal cannot be distinguished from noise in a bootstrapped permutation test (p>0.10).
